symptoms.symptom_name: Sustained regular palpitations / awareness of a fast heartbeat for ~12 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Regular sustained palpitations fit a sinus mechanism but are non-specific.
A regular palpitation is compatible with SVT; onset gradual rather than abrupt makes it less likely.
AF is classically irregularly irregular; a steady/regular pulse argues against it.
Patient-obtainable rate confirms tachycardia and regularity, but a wrist wearable cannot diagnose the underlying rhythm — admissible in telehealth, unverified.
A rate of ~128 is below the typical AVNRT/AVRT range of 150-220, making SVT somewhat less likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lightheadedness/presyncope on exertion, no loss of consciousness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Presyncope with a rapid rate can indicate haemodynamic compromise from a primary arrhythmia and warrants same-day assessment.
Mild lightheadedness is common with high adrenergic drive and dehydration; non-specific.
Fatigue after sleep deprivation, stress and a sustained high heart rate is expected but non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Atypical chest pressure/heaviness, non-crushing, no radiation to jaw or arm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Any chest discomfort with tachycardia requires ACS screening; the atypical, non-radiating character lowers but does not eliminate the concern.
Non-ischaemic chest pressure is common with anxiety and high adrenergic tone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked anxiety, restlessness and fear the heart is failing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute anxiety amplifies sympathetic drive and both causes and is caused by the tachycardia, feeding a symptom loop.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heavy energy-drink (caffeine) consumption over the last 48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A large acute caffeine load is a well-recognised direct driver of sinus tachycardia via sympathomimetic effect — the key exogenous cause here.
Acute psychological stress raises catecholamine drive, compounding the stimulant effect.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fine resting tremor of the outstretched hands with mild diaphoresis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fine tremor and sweating reflect adrenergic overdrive from stimulant and stress.
Tremor is also a thyrotoxic sign — a red herring that must be weighed against the absent goitre/weight loss and the clear stimulant history.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular rhythm on examination with bounding, easily palpable peripheral pulses; dual heart sounds, no murmur, no S3, no raised JVP; lungs clear

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A perfectly regular rhythm argues strongly against AF.
A regular hyperdynamic circulation with a structurally normal heart and no failure signs fits benign sinus tachycardia — but this is a sealed clinician examination the AI Doctor cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: No goitre on neck palpation; no weight loss; no exophthalmos reported

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of goitre, weight loss and eye signs lowers the probability of thyrotoxicosis, though TSH is still needed to exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever and no recent infective illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/illness lowers the likelihood of infection/sepsis as a secondary driver of the tachycardia.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG showing sinus rhythm at ~128 bpm (no flutter waves, sinus P before each QRS)

investigation_details.expected_result: Regular narrow-complex sinus tachycardia at ~128 bpm; normal P-wave axis and morphology; no flutter waves; no ischaemic ST/T changes
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without the ECG a primary tachyarrhythmia (SVT, atrial flutter) or ischaemia cannot be excluded and the benign label is unsafe.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sinus P waves preceding each QRS at the observed rate confirm sinus tachycardia and exclude SVT/flutter/AF — the decisive test, unavailable via telehealth.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Thyrotoxicosis remains formally unexcluded, though it is clinically unlikely here.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal TSH excludes thyrotoxicosis as the cause of the tachycardia and tremor.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sustained regular tachycardia + anxiety + tremor + diaphoresis on a background of heavy caffeine and acute stress is the classic sympathetic-overdrive picture of benign sinus tachycardia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The decisive discriminator between benign sinus tachycardia and a primary tachyarrhythmia (SVT, atrial flutter, AF) and a screen for ischaemia; must be obtained same-day in person because telehealth cannot provide it.
Excludes thyrotoxicosis; not urgent given the clear stimulant history and absent thyroid signs.
Screens for anaemia and electrolyte disturbance as secondary drivers of tachycardia.

Not indicated routinely for atypical, non-radiating chest pressure in a haemodynamically stable patient with a strong benign driver; reserve for the in-person assessment if the ECG or clinical picture raises ischaemic concern — do not order reflexively via telehealth.
Rhythm-terminating interventions must not be attempted remotely or before an ECG confirms a re-entrant SVT; inappropriate for a presumptive sinus tachycardia.
